title: Spinning platform
goal: Drive continuous motion from a Script with RunService.Heartbeat.

steps.body:
Game juice often comes from *always-on* motion — platforms that
rotate, signs that bob, dust that drifts. In Roblox, the
lowest-friction way to drive this is `RunService.Heartbeat`: a
signal that fires every server tick with a `deltaTime` you can
scale motion by.

Make a wide, flat, anchored Part at origin. Flat so the spin
reads clearly; anchored so physics doesn't interfere.

Add a Spinner Script that rotates the Platform by a fixed angular
rate per second. We multiply by `deltaTime` so the rate stays
consistent regardless of frame rate.
steps.explain:
`CFrame.Angles(0, angle, 0)` builds a rotation around the Y axis.
Multiplying the platform's existing CFrame by that rotation on the
right-hand side rotates it *about its own center*, not the world
origin.
steps.source:
local RunService = game:GetService("RunService")

local platform = script.Parent
local RADIANS_PER_SECOND = math.rad(45)

RunService.Heartbeat:Connect(function(deltaTime)
  platform.CFrame = platform.CFrame * CFrame.Angles(0, RADIANS_PER_SECOND * deltaTime, 0)
end)

steps.body:
Press ▶ **Play** (or F5). The Platform should rotate slowly in
place at ~45°/sec. If you applied the bob prompt, it'll also
float up and down gently. Shift+F5 to stop.
